description_long: This is a double-ended open wrench, commonly used for tightening or loosening nuts and bolts. The tool is made of a solid, dark metallic material, likely steel, and features two U-shaped open jaws at either end, each with a slightly different size. The body is flat and elongated, with a rectangular cross-section and a subtle taper toward each end. The surface appears matte with minimal gloss, and there are no visible rubber grips or ergonomic enhancements. The wrench is devoid of any significant decorative elements, with only faint markings or possible size indicators along the shaft. Its robust construction and utilitarian design make it suitable for heavy-duty mechanical or automotive tasks in urban environments such as garages or workshops.
description: A solid, double-ended open wrench made of dark metal with two U-shaped jaws and a flat, rectangular body. The surface is matte and utilitarian, with no visible grip enhancements.
description_view_0: The wrench is seen from the side, clearly showing both open jaws and the flat, elongated body.
description_view_1: The wrench is viewed from the end, revealing its thin, rectangular profile and the narrowness of the handle.
description_view_2: The opposite side view displays both open jaws and the flat body, mirroring view 0 but with the jaws reversed.
description_view_3: The wrench is seen from the other end, again showing the thin profile and the width of the handle.
